name: Methi Thepla
tagline: Gujarati fenugreek-flatbread — blood-sugar friendly travel staple
highlight: Fenugreek's galactomannan fiber slows glucose absorption — ideal for HbA1c control

hero.alt: Stack of green-flecked fenugreek theplas on a wooden board with a side of yogurt
hero.caption: methi thepla — fenugreek-flecked flatbread

healthFacts: Fenugreek's soluble fiber lowers post-meal glucose spikes by 20–30%.
Whole-wheat + besan combo gives complete protein and slow-release carbs.
Fenugreek leaves contain iron, vitamin K, and modest amounts of folate.
Cooks dry on a tawa with minimal oil — friendly to LDL and triglyceride control.

steps.title: Knead the dough
steps.detail: Combine all dough ingredients and knead with a little water until smooth and pliable. Rest 15 minutes covered.
steps.tip: The dough should be slightly stiffer than a chapati dough — keeps theplas thin.

steps.title: Roll thin
steps.detail: Divide into 10 balls. Roll each into a thin round (~6 inches) on a lightly floured surface.
steps.tip: Thin theplas mean a longer shelf life — they keep for 2–3 days at room temperature.

steps.title: Cook on tawa
steps.detail: Heat a tawa on medium. Cook each thepla 30 seconds per side, then brush both sides with a little oil and cook another 30 seconds till golden brown spots appear.
steps.tip: Eat with thick yogurt and pickle for the canonical pairing.
